intro: Malveopsis fragrans is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Malvaceae family, known for its sweetly fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Malveopsis fragrans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, skin infections, and digestive problems.
